The estimates are based on the main effect of dry spell days with 28+ day durations. The analysis also considers interaction effects with heat waves, though this specific value is focused on the direct impact of dry spell days alone.

The table on this page reports the marginal effects of an additional dry spell day on crop production for different minimum durations defining dry spells and heat waves. Significant effects at 5% level are noted for dry spells of 28 days and beyond across heat wave durations. The analysis is based on data up to 2016.
Effect of an additional dry spell day on crop production in the current year. Significant at p<0.05.
